pattern: After sorting, the best target is always some element, and a sliding window bounded by the cost to raise everyone inside it to the window's right end finds the longest affordable run.
hints: It is never worse to make the chosen target one of the array values, and you only ever raise smaller numbers up to it.
Sort, then slide a window where the cost to lift everything in it to the rightmost value stays within k.
As the right edge advances, cost grows by adding the new element times the window width difference; shrink from the left while target*width - windowSum exceeds k, tracking the max width.

edge_inputs.description: budget too small to merge anything

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [nums, k]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxFrequency(self, nums, k):
        # for each candidate right endpoint, greedily extend left as long as
        # the prefix lift cost fits; O(n) two-pointer but written independently
        a = sorted(nums)
        n = len(a)
        best = 1
        left = 0
        total = 0
        for right in range(n):
            total += a[right]
            while a[right] * (right - left + 1) - total > k:
                total -= a[left]
                left += 1
            best = max(best, right - left + 1)
        return best
